title: Translate reviewed seed glossary into first target language with back-translation QA
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual translates reviewed oncology glossary entries into under-served languages. This task produces the first translated language set from the reviewed seed glossary (glossary-source-001), using the termbase (termbase-001), with qualified medical-translation reviewer sign-off and mandatory back-translation QA for all safety-load terms before any translation ships.
objective: Translate all reviewed source terms (from glossary-source-001) into the first target language using termbase-001, preserving meaning, negations, urgency, and preserved terms. Obtain qualified medical-translation reviewer sign-off on each entry. Perform mandatory back-translation QA for every term with safetyLoad other than none.
acceptanceCriteria: Every reviewed and signed-off source term from glossary-source-001 is translated into the target language in glossary/<lang>/<term-id>.yaml, preserving meaning, negations, urgency, and preserved terms from termbase-001
Qualified medical-translation reviewer sign-off (translators-001) is recorded per entry; reviewer is independent of the drafting step and COI-declared

Back-translation QA is performed for every term with safetyLoad other than none: an independent back-translation is produced and compared to the reviewed source definition before sign-off; any discrepancy is resolved before the entry is marked done
All agent uncertainty flags are resolved or accepted-as-is with reasoning before sign-off
Each translation inherits source attribution and the not-medical-advice notice in the target language
license-002 passes for every translated entry (attribution, provenance, notMedicalAdvice:true, license-compatible)
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach Stage B section
output: glossary/<lang>/<term-id>.yaml for every reviewed source term, with translation review sign-offs and back-translation QA results recorded
